name: pyogrio (Fast GDAL/OGR Vector I/O for Python)

performance.notes: 5-10x faster than Fiona for large files; GDAL C++ backend; vectorized I/O

agent_readiness.notes: Fast GDAL-backed vector I/O, GeoPandas' default engine since 0.14. import pyogrio. gdf = pyogrio.read_dataframe('data.gpkg'). pyogrio.write_dataframe(gdf, 'output.gpkg'). info = pyogrio.read_info('data.gpkg'). pyogrio.list_layers('data.gpkg'). Bbox filter: pyogrio.read_dataframe('data.gpkg', bbox=(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)). SQL filter: where='population > 1000000'. Arrow-based: use_arrow=True for 2-3x speedup. Works with GeoPandas engine='pyogrio'. Formats: Shapefile, GeoPackage, GeoJSON, FlatGeobuf, FileGDB.

gotchas: Requires GDAL libraries (bundled in conda; manual install on some systems)
use_arrow=True requires pyarrow; further speedup but requires understanding Arrow
FileGDB (Esri .gdb) requires optional OpenFileGDB or ESRI FileGDB driver
Not a drop-in replacement for Fiona's feature iteration pattern
best_when: Fast reading/writing of large vector datasets (Shapefile, GeoPackage, FlatGeobuf)
Spatial and attribute filtering during read (bbox, where)
When Fiona is too slow for large files
avoid_when: When GDAL dependencies can't be installed
Very small datasets where speed doesn't matter
summary: pyogrio provides fast GDAL-backed vector I/O for Python, default GeoPandas engine since 0.14. pyogrio.read_dataframe('data.gpkg', bbox=..., where='...'). write_dataframe(). use_arrow=True for 2-3x speedup. 5-10x faster than Fiona. MIT by GeoPandas contributors. Best for large Shapefile/GeoPackage/FlatGeobuf workflows. Supports spatial and attribute filtering on read. FileGDB needs optional driver.
